016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.math.*;
023import java.nio.charset.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
026
027/**
028 * Specialized input stream for parsing BSON (Binary JSON) streams.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * BSON uses little-endian byte order for all multi-byte values.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
034 *    <li class='note'>
035 *       This class is not intended for external use.
036 * </ul>
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
039 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/BsonBasics">BSON Basics</a>
040 * </ul>
041 */
042public class BsonInputStream extends ParserInputStream {
043
044   private static final Charset UTF8 = StandardCharsets.UTF_8;
045   private static final String UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM = "Unexpected end of BSON stream";
046
047   /** Default cap (16 MiB, the conventional BSON document-size limit) for wire-declared lengths. */
048   static final int DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH = 16 * 1024 * 1024;
049
050   private int pushback = -1;
051   private int maxLength = DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH;
052
053   /**
054    * Constructor.
055    *
056    * @param pipe The parser input.
057    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
058    */
059   protected BsonInputStream(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
060      super(pipe);
061   }
062
063   /**
064    * Sets the maximum allowed wire-declared length (in bytes) for strings, binary payloads, and document/array
065    * size prefixes.
066    *
067    * <p>
068    * Guards against malformed/adversarial input where a small payload declares a huge or negative length that
069    * would otherwise trigger {@link OutOfMemoryError} or {@link NegativeArraySizeException}.
070    *
071    * @param value The maximum length in bytes.  Values &le; 0 disable the cap (only the negative-length check remains).
072    */
073   void setMaxLength(int value) {
074      maxLength = value <= 0 ? Integer.MAX_VALUE : value;
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Validates a wire-declared length against sane bounds before it is used to allocate a buffer.
079    *
080    * @param len The declared length read off the wire.
081    * @param what A short description of the field being read (for the error message).
082    * @return The validated length.
083    * @throws IOException If the length is negative or exceeds the configured maximum.
084    */
085   private int checkLength(int len, String what) throws IOException {
086      if (len < 0)
087         throw ioex("Invalid BSON {0} length (negative): {1}", what, len);
088      if (len > maxLength)
089         throw ioex("BSON {0} length {1} exceeds maximum allowed {2}", what, len, maxLength);
090      return len;
091   }
092
093   @Override
094   public int read() throws IOException {
095      if (pushback >= 0) {
096         var b = pushback;
097         pushback = -1;
098         return b;
099      }
100      return super.read();
101   }
102
103   /**
104    * Pushes back one byte for the next read.
105    */
106   private void pushBack(int b) {
107      pushback = b & 0xFF;
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Reads a 4-byte little-endian integer.
112    *
113    * @return The 32-bit value.
114    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
115    */
116   public int readLE4() throws IOException {
117      var b1 = read();
118      var b2 = read();
119      var b3 = read();
120      var b4 = read();
121      if (b4 < 0)
122         throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
123      return (b1 & 0xFF) | ((b2 & 0xFF) << 8) | ((b3 & 0xFF) << 16) | ((b4 & 0xFF) << 24);
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Reads an 8-byte little-endian long.
128    *
129    * @return The 64-bit value.
130    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
131    */
132   public long readLE8() throws IOException {
133      var lo = readLE4() & 0xFFFFFFFFL;
134      var hi = readLE4() & 0xFFFFFFFFL;
135      return lo | (hi << 32);
136   }
137
138   /**
139    * Reads the document size (int32) from the stream.
140    *
141    * @return The document size in bytes.
142    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
143    */
144   public int readDocumentSize() throws IOException {
145      return checkLength(readLE4(), "document");
146   }
147
148   /**
149    * Reads the 1-byte element type.
150    *
151    * @return The BSON type byte (0x01-0x13, 0x7F, 0xFF).
152    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
153    */
154   public int readElementType() throws IOException {
155      var b = read();
156      if (b < 0)
157         throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
158      return b & 0xFF;
159   }
160
161   /**
162    * Reads a cstring (null-terminated UTF-8).
163    *
164    * @return The element name.
165    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
166    */
167   public String readElementName() throws IOException {
168      return readCString();
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Reads a cstring (UTF-8 bytes until 0x00).
173    *
174    * @return The string value.
175    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
176    */
177   public String readCString() throws IOException {
178      var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
179      int b;
180      while ((b = read()) >= 0 && b != 0)
181         baos.write(b);
182      if (b < 0)
183         throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
184      return new String(baos.toByteArray(), UTF8);
185   }
186
187   /**
188    * Returns whether the next byte is the document terminator (0x00).
189    *
190    * @return <jk>true</jk> if next byte is 0x00, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
191    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
192    */
193   public boolean isDocumentEnd() throws IOException {
194      var b = read();
195      if (b >= 0)
196         pushBack(b);
197      return b == 0x00;
198   }
199
200   /**
201    * Consumes the document terminator byte (0x00).
202    *
203    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely or byte is not 0x00.
204    */
205   public void readDocumentTerminator() throws IOException {
206      var b = read();
207      if (b != 0x00)
208         throw ioex("Expected document terminator, got {0}", b);
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Reads an IEEE 754 double (8 bytes, little-endian).
213    *
214    * @return The double value.
215    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
216    */
217   public double readDouble() throws IOException {
218      return Double.longBitsToDouble(readLE8());
219   }
220
221   /**
222    * Reads a 32-bit integer (4 bytes, little-endian).
223    *
224    * @return The integer value.
225    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
226    */
227   public int readInt32() throws IOException {
228      return readLE4();
229   }
230
231   /**
232    * Reads a 64-bit integer (8 bytes, little-endian).
233    *
234    * @return The long value.
235    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
236    */
237   public long readInt64() throws IOException {
238      return readLE8();
239   }
240
241   /**
242    * Reads a boolean (1 byte: 0x00=false, 0x01=true).
243    *
244    * @return The boolean value.
245    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
246    */
247   public boolean readBoolean() throws IOException {
248      var b = read();
249      if (b < 0)
250         throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
251      return b != 0;
252   }
253
254   /**
255    * Reads a BSON string (int32 length + UTF-8 + 0x00).
256    *
257    * @return The string value.
258    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
259    */
260   public String readString() throws IOException {
261      var len = readLE4();
262      if (len <= 0)
263         throw ioex("Invalid BSON string length: {0}", len);
264      checkLength(len, "string");
265      var bytes = new byte[len - 1]; // length includes null
266      for (var i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) {
267         var b = read();
268         if (b < 0)
269            throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
270         bytes[i] = (byte)b;
271      }
272      var term = read();
273      if (term != 0x00)
274         throw ioex("Expected string terminator, got {0}", term);
275      return new String(bytes, UTF8);
276   }
277
278   /**
279    * Reads BSON binary (int32 length + subtype + bytes).
280    *
281    * @return The binary data.
282    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
283    */
284   public byte[] readBinary() throws IOException {
285      var len = checkLength(readLE4(), "binary");
286      var subtype = read();
287      if (subtype < 0)
288         throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
289      var bytes = new byte[len];
290      for (var i = 0; i < len; i++) {
291         var b = read();
292         if (b < 0)
293            throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
294         bytes[i] = (byte)b;
295      }
296      return bytes;
297   }
298
299   /**
300    * Reads BSON datetime (int64, UTC millis since epoch).
301    *
302    * @return The UTC millis since epoch.
303    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
304    */
305   public long readDateTime() throws IOException {
306      return readLE8();
307   }
308
309   /**
310    * Reads ObjectId (12 bytes) as 24-character hex string.
311    *
312    * @return The ObjectId as hex string.
313    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
314    */
315   public String readObjectId() throws IOException {
316      var bytes = new byte[12];
317      for (var i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
318         var b = read();
319         if (b < 0)
320            throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
321         bytes[i] = (byte)b;
322      }
323      var sb = new StringBuilder(24);
324      for (var b : bytes)
325         sb.append(String.format("%02x", b & 0xFF));
326      return sb.toString();
327   }
328
329   /**
330    * Reads BSON decimal128 (16 bytes, IEEE 754-2008) as BigDecimal.
331    *
332    * @return The decimal value, or <jk>null</jk> if the wire value is NaN or ±Infinity (which have no BigDecimal form).
333    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
334    */
335   public BigDecimal readDecimal128() throws IOException {
336      var bytes = new byte[16];
337      for (var i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
338         var b = read();
339         if (b < 0)
340            throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
341         bytes[i] = (byte)b;
342      }
343      return decodeDecimal128(bytes);
344   }
345
346   /**
347    * Skips a value of the given BSON type.
348    *
349    * @param type The BSON type byte (0x01-0x13, 0x7F, 0xFF).
350    * @throws IOException If the stream ends prematurely.
351    */
352   public void skipValue(int type) throws IOException {
353      switch (type) {
354         case 0x01 -> readLE8();
355         case 0x02 -> {
356            var len = readLE4();
357            skip(len);
358         }
359         case 0x03, 0x04 -> {
360            var size = readLE4();
361            skip(size - 4);
362         }
363         case 0x05 -> {
364            var len = readLE4();
365            read();
366            skip(len);
367         }
368         case 0x06 -> { /* BSON Undefined (deprecated) - zero bytes, intentionally ignored */ }
369         case 0x07 -> skip(12);
370         case 0x08 -> read();
371         case 0x09 -> readLE8();
372         case 0x0A -> { /* BSON Null - zero bytes, no payload to consume */ }
373         case 0x0B -> {
374            readCString();
375            readCString();
376         }
377         case 0x0C -> {
378            readString();
379            skip(12);
380         }
381         case 0x0D -> readString();
382         case 0x0E -> readString();
383         case 0x0F -> {
384            var len = readLE4();
385            skip(len - 4);
386         }
387         case 0x10 -> readLE4();
388         case 0x11 -> readLE8();
389         case 0x12 -> readLE8();
390         case 0x13 -> skip(16);
391         case 0x7F, 0xFF -> { /* BSON MinKey/MaxKey - zero bytes, marker types with no payload to consume */ }
392         default -> throw ioex("Unknown BSON type: {0}", type);
393      }
394   }
395
396   private void skip(int n) throws IOException {
397      for (var i = 0; i < n; i++)
398         if (read() < 0)
399            throw ioex(UNEXPECTED_END_OF_BSON_STREAM);
400   }
401
402   /**
403    * Decodes IEEE 754-2008 decimal128 bytes to BigDecimal.
404    *
405    * <p>
406    * Bytes are little-endian: bytes 0-7 = low 64 bits, bytes 8-15 = high 64 bits.
407    * Matches {@link BsonOutputStream#writeDecimal128} output.
408    */
409   private static BigDecimal decodeDecimal128(byte[] bytes) {
410      if (bytes.length != 16)
411         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Decimal128 must be 16 bytes");
412      var low = readLE8From(bytes, 0);
413      var high = readLE8From(bytes, 8);
414      // NaN/Infinity are valid Decimal128 wire values (MongoDB emits them) but have no BigDecimal form; normalize
415      // them to null instead of throwing an unchecked ArithmeticException that would abort the whole parse.
416      return BsonDecimal128.fromIEEE754BIDEncoding(high, low).toBigDecimalOrNull();
417   }
418
419   private static long readLE8From(byte[] bytes, int offset) {
420      return ((long)(bytes[offset + 7] & 0xFF) << 56) | ((long)(bytes[offset + 6] & 0xFF) << 48)
421         | ((long)(bytes[offset + 5] & 0xFF) << 40) | ((long)(bytes[offset + 4] & 0xFF) << 32)
422         | ((long)(bytes[offset + 3] & 0xFF) << 24) | ((long)(bytes[offset + 2] & 0xFF) << 16)
423         | ((long)(bytes[offset + 1] & 0xFF) << 8) | (bytes[offset] & 0xFF);
424   }
425}
